1.description:
The displacement field of the wave equation
JUSTIFICATION:
Explicitly defined as the dependent variable in the wave equation and appears in multiple forms such as u(t, x) and its derivatives.

10.description:
Volume element of integration over the domain Ω
JUSTIFICATION:
Appears in integrals over Ω such as ∫_Ω ρ ∂²u/∂t² w dΩ and ∫_Ω T ∇u ⋅ ∇w dΩ.

11.description:
Surface element of integration over the boundary Γ
JUSTIFICATION:
Appears in integrals over Γ such as ∫_Γ [T ∂n u] w dΓ and ∫_∂Ω g w dΓ.

12.description:
Neumann boundary condition on the boundary ∂Ω_N
JUSTIFICATION:
Defined as the boundary condition on ∂Ω_N in equation (4b) and appears in the weak form as a source term.

13.description:
Temporal domain of integration from 0 to t_end
JUSTIFICATION:
Defined as the script T = [0, t_end] and used in the weak formulation to specify the time interval over which the solution is defined.

2.description:
The test function used in the weak formulation of the wave equation
JUSTIFICATION:
Appears repeatedly as a test function in the weak form equations (7) and (8), and is required to belong to the Sobolev space H^1_0(Ω).

3.description:
Mass per unit volume of the medium
JUSTIFICATION:
Defined as the positive mass per unit volume in the wave equation and used in the time derivative term of the equation.

4.description:
Tensile pre-stress as load per unit surface area
JUSTIFICATION:
Defined as the positive tensile pre-stress in the wave equation and appears in the stress term involving the gradient of displacement.

5.description:
The main spatial computational domain in d-dimensional space
JUSTIFICATION:
Defined as a d-dimensional spatial domain Ω ⊂ R^d and appears in multiple contexts including integration domains and subdomain partitions.

6.description:
The collection of interfaces between subdomains
JUSTIFICATION:
Defined as the interface between subdomains Ω_i and appears in transmission conditions (5a) and (5b).

7.description:
Temporal variable representing time
JUSTIFICATION:
Used as the time variable in the wave equation and appears in time derivatives and in the temporal domain T = [0, t_end].

8.description:
Space of functions with continuous first derivatives in time and spatial Sobolev space H^1_0(Ω)
JUSTIFICATION:
Defined as H^1(T; H^1_0(Ω)) and used to describe the regularity of the solution in the temporal domain.

9.description:
Dual space of H^1_0(Ω) used for duality pairing
JUSTIFICATION:
Defined as H^{-1}(Ω) and used in the duality pairing between the time derivative term and the test function space.
